perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall, upon termination of this Agreement, or upon written request of Discloser, whichever is earlier, immediately, but not later than 10 days after any notice thereof by Discloser, return (or destroy at Discloser’s option) all copies of such Discloser’s Confidential Information and certify in writing its compliance with this requirement, except that Recipient may retain a copy of such Confidential Information solely for archival purpose .
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall, upon termination of this Agreement, or upon written request of Discloser, whichever is earlier, return (or destroy at Discloser’s option) all copies of such Discloser’s Confidential Information, except that Recipient may retain a copy of such Confidential Information solely for archival purpose.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "immediately, but not later than 10 days after any notice thereof by Discloser, and certify in writing its compliance with this requirement", the modified text no longer specifies a deadline for returning or destroying Confidential Information and providing certification. Section 8 states that the confidentiality obligations survive termination, but if the return isn't time-bound and certified, it's difficult to enforce. This weakens the enforceability of the return obligation, creating an in-text contradiction due to the missing requirement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term and Termination . This Agreement shall be effective from the Effective Date until 24 months thereafter. Either party may terminate this Agreement for any or no reason upon 10 days written notice to the other party. However, any termination of this Agreement shall not relieve Recipient of its confidentiality and use obligations with respec t to Confidential Information disclosed prior to the date of such termination. Except for the right to use Confidential Information for the Purpose, which right terminates when this Agreement terminates, Recipient’s duty to protect Discloser’s Confidential Information expires 5 years from the date on which that Confidential Information was disclosed to Recipient. Sections 4, 5, 7, 8, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 and 16 shall survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a deadline ('immediately, but not later than 10 days after any notice') for returning confidential information and requires written certification. The changed text removes this deadline and certification requirement. Section 8 (Contradicted Text) states that confidentiality obligations survive termination, but without a defined timeframe and certification, enforcing the return of confidential information becomes problematic, creating a direct conflict and contradiction. The lack of a deadline and certification in the changed text directly contradicts the implicit assumption in the contradicted section that the return of information is enforceable and time-bound.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall comply with all applicable US and other export laws, regulations and rules and, in particular, will not export or re -export Confidential Information without obtaining all required government licenses, approvals or waivers .
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall comply with all applicable laws, regulations and rules.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 10 specifically prohibits exporting or re-exporting Confidential Information without proper government licenses. The modified text removes this specific restriction. This creates a contradiction because Section 2 obligates protection of Confidential Information, yet this change removes a critical control on potential disclosure routes (export). The level of due care expected in Section 2 is not defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nondisclosure and Nonuse Obligations . Each of the parties, as Recipient , hereby promise s and agree s to receive and hold Confidential Information in confidence, and to protect and safeguard Confidential Information against unauthorized use or disclosure using at least the same degree of care as Recipient accords to its own confidential information of lik e importance, but in no case less than reasonable care. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, each party, as Recipient , further promise s and agree s: (a) not to, directly or indirectly, in any way, disclose, make accessible, reveal, report, pub lish, disseminate or transfer any Confidential Inf ormation to any third party; (b) not to use any Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, except in furtherance of the Purpose in acc ordance with this Agreement; and (c) to restrict access to Confi dential Information to those of its officers, directors and employees who have a legitimate need - to-know to carry out the Purpose and who are obligated to protect such Confidential Information pursuant to terms and conditions no less protective of Disclose r than those contained in this Agreement ; and (d) not to reproduce or copy Confidential Information except to the extent necessary to further the Purpose . Confidential Furthermore, the existence of any business negotiati ons, discussions or agreements in progress between the parties shall be kept confidential and shall not be disclosed without written approval of all the parties . For the purposes of this Agreement, Company shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other division, department, group, or unit other than Samsung KNOX Business and Technology Division .
perturbation.justification: The original text specifically prohibited exporting confidential information without government licenses. The changed text removes this specific prohibition, only requiring compliance with all applicable laws, regulations and rules. Section 2 obligates protection of Confidential Information using at least reasonable care. Removing the export restriction in the changed text creates a contradiction because Section 2's due care expected is undefined, yet the original restriction specifically ensured confidential information would not be exported without proper licenses. The modified text therefore contradicts the level of protection that Section 2 implies, creating a conflict. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: All disputes, controversies or claims between the parties arising out of or in connection with this Agreement (including its existence, validity or termination) shall be finally resolved by arbitration to be held in Seoul, Korea and conducted in English under the Rules of Arbitration of the Intern ational Chamber of Commerce ; provided, however, that each party may enforce its or its affiliates’ intellectual property rights in any court of competent jurisdiction, including but not limit ed to equitable relief .
perturbation.changed_text: All disputes, controversies or claims between the parties arising out of or in connection with this Agreement (including its existence, validity or termination) shall be finally resolved by arbitration to be held in Seoul, Korea and conducted in English under the Rules of Arbitration of the Intern ational Chamber of Commerce ;
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 15 allowed each party to enforce its intellectual property rights in any court of competent jurisdiction, including equitable relief. By removing this provision, the modified text now mandates arbitration for *all* disputes, including IP disputes. This contradicts the standard practice of pursuing IP enforcement through courts to obtain injunctive relief quickly. The removal creates a contradiction between the agreement's intent to protect Confidential Information, which includes IP, and the means to protect it, because enforcing IP rights through arbitration is slower and less effective for stopping ongoing infringement. The original section granted the company the power to file a lawsuit to immediately cease IP infringement but that power has now been revoked.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges that any disclosure, use or misappropriation of Confidential Inform ation of another party in violation of this Agreement would cause such party irreparable harm for which there may be no adequate remedy at law. Accordingly, each party agrees that such other party shall have the right to apply to any court of competent jur isdiction for injunctive relief and specific performance, without prejudice to any remedies available to it at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for intellectual property rights to be enforced in any court of competent jurisdiction, while the changed text mandates arbitration for all disputes, including those related to IP. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which grants each party the right to seek injunctive relief in court for misappropriation of confidential information (which includes IP). The change removes the ability to quickly seek injunctive relief, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The English language text of this Agreement shall prevail over any translations thereof. If a contract is entered into by the parties in respect of the Purpose, this Agreement shall be deemed incorporated into such contract in addition to any confidential ity obligations set forth in such contract, if any , and the term of this Agreement is deemed to be extended (but not shorted) to be the same as that of the contract .
perturbation.changed_text: The English language text of this Agreement shall prevail over any translations thereof. If a contract is entered into by the parties in respect of the Purpose, this Agreement shall be deemed incorporated into such contract in addition to any confidential ity obligations set forth in such contract, if any ,.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "and the term of this Agreement is deemed to be extended (but not shorted) to be the same as that of the contract", the modified text creates uncertainty regarding the term of the agreement if another contract is entered into. Section 8 specifies a 24-month term, but without the extension clause, it's unclear if that term remains in effect if a subsequent contract exists. The explicit statement about extending the term in the instance of another contract being signed has now been omitted. By the terms of the original agreement, the Confidentiality clause remains active for the length of the hypothetical second contract. The change creates an internal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term and Termination . This Agreement shall be effective from the Effective Date until 24 months thereafter. Either party may terminate this Agreement for any or no reason upon 10 days written notice to the other party. However, any termination of this Agreement shall not relieve Recipient of its confidentiality and use obligations with respec t to Confidential Information disclosed prior to the date of such termination. Except for the right to use Confidential Information for the Purpose, which right terminates when this Agreement terminates, Recipient’s duty to protect Discloser’s Confidential Information expires 5 years from the date on which that Confidential Information was disclosed to Recipient. Sections 4, 5, 7, 8, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 and 16 shall survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that the term of the agreement would extend to match that of any subsequent contract related to the Purpose. By removing this clause, the modified text now contradicts the 'Term and Termination' section which specifies a fixed 24-month term. The omission creates uncertainty about the agreement's duration if another contract is signed, directly conflicting with the fixed term stated in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient may disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information to Recipient’s affiliates on the condition that Recipient shall restrict access to Discloser’s Confidential Information to those of Recipient’s affiliates’ officers, directors and employees who have a legitimate need -to-know to carry out the Purpose and who are obligated to protect such Confidential Information pursuant to terms and conditions no less protective of Discloser than those contained in this Agreement and Recipient shall be liable for any failure of its affiliates to abide by the provisions of this Agreement as if such failure was the act or omiss ion of such party.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information to Recipient’s affiliates on the condition that Recipient shall restrict access to Discloser’s Confidential Information to those of Recipient’s affiliates’ officers, directors and employees who have a legitimate need -to-know to carry out the Purpose .
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "and who are obligated to protect such Confidential Information pursuant to terms and conditions no less protective of Discloser than those contained in this Agreement and Recipient shall be liable for any failure of its affiliates to abide by the provisions of this Agreement as if such failure was the act or omiss ion of such party", the modified text creates uncertainty regarding the affiliates needing to be obligated to protect Confidential Information under the terms of this agreement, and if Recipient remains liable for affiliate actions. This weakens the enforceability of the confidentiality obligations, creating an in-text contradiction in that they are able to disclose to affiliates with no responsibility. Section 2 and Section 11 which require strict adherence and acknowledgment that a breach could lead to irreparable harm, are now contradicated in that the recipient can disclose to a subsidiary with no oversight
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nondisclosure and Nonuse Obligations . Each of the parties, as Recipient , hereby promise s and agree s to receive and hold Confidential Information in confidence, and to protect and safeguard Confidential Information against unauthorized use or disclosure using at least the same degree of care as Recipient accords to its own confidential information of lik e importance, but in no case less than reasonable care. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, each party, as Recipient , further promise s and agree s: (a) not to, directly or indirectly, in any way, disclose, make accessible, reveal, report, pub lish, disseminate or transfer any Confidential Inf ormation to any third party; (b) not to use any Confidential Information in any manner whatsoever, except in furtherance of the Purpose in acc ordance with this Agreement; and (c) to restrict access to Confi dential Information to those of its officers, directors and employees who have a legitimate need - to-know to carry out the Purpose and who are obligated to protect such Confidential Information pursuant to terms and conditions no less protective of Disclose r than those contained in this Agreement ; and (d) not to reproduce or copy Confidential Information except to the extent necessary to further the Purpose . Confidential Furthermore, the existence of any business negotiati ons, discussions or agreements in progress between the parties shall be kept confidential and shall not be disclosed without written approval of all the parties . For the purposes of this Agreement, Company shall not disclose Confidential Information to any other division, department, group, or unit other than Samsung KNOX Business and Technology Division .
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that Recipient's affiliates must be obligated to protect Confidential Information under terms no less protective than those in the agreement, and that the Recipient is liable for affiliate breaches. The changed text removes this obligation and liability clause. The contradicted text emphasizes the Recipient's obligation to protect Confidential Information, including restricting access to affiliates obligated to protect it under similar terms. The removal of the affiliate obligation in the changed text directly contradicts the explicit requirements outlined in the contradicted text, creating a strong in-text contradiction because the Recipient can now disclose to affiliates with no oversight or responsibility, which contrasts with the strict nondisclosure obligations in the contradicted text.
